body:
Title: CRA Form NB428 and Worksheet NB428 2025 New Brunswick resident tax.

Source:
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5004-c/5004-c-25e.txt
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5004-c/5004-c-25e.pdf
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5004-d/5004-d-25e.txt
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5004-d/5004-d-25e.pdf

CRA Form NB428 2025 calculates New Brunswick tax for New Brunswick residents. Part A calculates New Brunswick tax on taxable income from line 26000. The bracket thresholds are $51,306, $102,614, and $190,060. The rates are 9.4%, 14%, 16%, and 19.5%. The base tax amounts are $0.00, $4,822.76, $12,005.88, and $25,997.24.

Part B calculates New Brunswick non-refundable tax credits. Line 58040 basic personal amount is $13,396. Worksheet NB428 line 58080 age amount has a maximum of $6,037, starts reducing above $44,945, is zero at $85,192 or more, and uses a 15% reduction rate. Lines 58120 and 58160 use a base amount of $11,550 and cap the amount at $10,499. Line 58360 pension income amount is capped at $1,000. Line 58440 disability amount for self is $10,010 unless the under-18 worksheet applies. Line 58689 medical expenses are reduced by the lesser of $2,798 and 3% of net income, then medical expenses for other dependants line 58729 are added. Line 58840 multiplies the total claim amount by 9.4%. Donations and gifts line 58969 multiplies federal Schedule 9 line 13 by 9.4% and line 14 by 17.95%. Line 61500 adds line 58840 and line 58969 as New Brunswick non-refundable tax credits.

Part C calculates New Brunswick tax. Line 56 adds tax on taxable income and tax on split income line 61510. Worksheet NB428 line 61520 calculates the dividend tax credit by multiplying taxable dividends other than eligible dividends line 12010 by 2.75% and taxable dividends line 12000 minus line 12010 by 14%. Lines 61 and 64 multiply minimum tax amounts by 57%. Lines 68 to 90 calculate New Brunswick low-income tax reduction: the basic reduction is $802, spouse and eligible dependant reductions are $802 each with a $1,604 subtotal cap, eligibility uses $48,653 for a single taxpayer and $75,387 for a taxpayer with a spouse or eligible dependant, adjusted family income above $21,920 reduces the amount at 3%. Worksheet NB428 line 92 calculates the political contribution credit using thresholds of $200, $550, and more than $1,075, rates of 75%, 50%, and 33.33%, base credits of $150 and $325, and a maximum credit of $500. Line 61670 caps the labour-sponsored venture capital fund tax credit at $2,000. Line 96 caps the small business investor tax credit at $125,000. Line 97 enters New Brunswick tax on line 42800.
